title: cerastium semidecandrum subsp. balearicum
intro: Cerastium semidecandrum subsp. balearicum, also known as Balearic mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its small, white, and pink flowers. Traditionally, Balearic mouse-ear chickweed has been used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Balearic mouse-ear chickweed can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or topical application of its leaves and flowers.
